channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: College Football 27 And Madden 27 Reveal Coming Next Week
item.content:encoded:
It’s time to get aboard the EA Sports football gaming hype train. The company has announced that it will be revealing both College Football 27 and Madden NFL 27 next week.

On June 4, EA Sports will put on its new “Opening Drive” showcase in what will be a new way to reveal the games.

“EA Sports Opening Drive brings together the full vision for College Football and Madden NFL in one connected showcase, covering every mode, every major innovation, and every way to play,” the company said.

As far as what will be revealed, that remains unknown. Insider Gaming understands that EA Sports is having a special event in the near future in Chicago for Madden NFL, which could suggest that the game’s cover athlete this year will be Chicago Bears quarterback Caleb Williams. And while there are rumors, such as College Football possibly coming to PC, nothing has been confirmed at this point.

What do you think of the new way EA Sports is revealing College Football 27 and Madden NFL 27? Leave your thoughts on the annual event in the comments below, or join the discussion in the official Insider Gaming Discord.

In other news, check out everything that was announced as part of the inaugural Insider Gaming Showcase on May 28, including 15 world premieres. And for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter.

Mike has been covering the gaming industry since 2012, and has reported on some of the largest events in the industry while also working as an investigative reporter. Outside of…

More from Mike Straw

item.title: XBOX Game Pass Price Drop Has Been Beneficial, Says CEO
item.content:encoded:
The new XBOX CEO has revealed that the Game Pass price reduction has been ‘a good first step’ for the company.

Towards the end of last year, it was announced that the price of XBOX Game Pass Ultimate would increase by 50%, and the decision didn’t sit well with the community. Since then, the new CEO has stepped in, and one of her first decisions has been to decrease the price, which a whole lot of players have appreciated.

Game Pass Price Drop Has Been Good, But There’s Still a Way to Go

A report by Verge (via Gamerant) reveals an internal memo from XBOX CEO Asha Sharma detailing how the Game Pass Price Drop has impacted the company. The CEO has stated that the price reductions have led to growth in acquisitions and improved retention for the subscription service. The memo didn’t include any statistics on how many new customers they have acquired. Check out the statement below:

Since our price reduction, we have seen acquisitions grow and retention improve, which is a good first step. We will not solve this in one moment or one launch.

The CEO added that the problem hasn’t been solved completely, and some time and some resolutions remain before they can be on the “path to restore durable growth.” For those unaware, if players tried to subscribe to XBOX Game Pass Ultimate before the price drop, it would have cost them $29.99 per month. Since the price has been reduced, it now costs players $22.99 per month. The CEO decreased the price as it felt “too expensive”.

In other news, a host of upcoming games have been added to XBOX Game Pass Coming Soon, suggesting they might appear at the upcoming showcase. Additionally, EA ‘hated’ the idea of a Family Tier of XBOX Game Pass, ultimately leading Microsoft to scrap development. What are your thoughts on the Game Pass price drop being good for the company? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about ARC Raiders finally winning a Game of the Year award. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: Official Summer Game Fest 2026 Event Calendar—All Shows, Dates, Times & Schedule
item.content:encoded:
The Summer Game Fest 2026 calendar is very busy. There are countless broadcasts taking place over the course of just a few days, and it’s easy to lose track of which shows are happening on which days. Insider Gaming’s Summer Game Fest 2026 calendar is something you need to bookmark.

Many don’t know that more happens during Summer Game Fest week other than the main showcase!

There are many showcases, presentations, and directs happening throughout the week, unveiling indie games and upcoming AAA beasts. There’s so much to soak in about SGF 2026 week, so let’s get into it.

Full Summer Game Fest 2026 Presentation Schedule

Between Sony State of Play, Summer Game Fest Showcase, Days of the Devs, and more, check out all the different events and when they’re happening.

  • More Insider Gaming: Insider Gaming Showcase—Everything Announced

Make sure to click on the Sony State of Play, Summer Game Fest Showcase 2026, Xbox Games Showcase & Gears of War: E-Day Direct, and Nintendo Direct links in the table below for more details on each!

Are you already noting down which shows and presentations you’re interested in? Let us know which ones you intend to watch via the Insider Gaming Discord.

Check out the Best Sony PlayStation Days of Play 2026 Deals: End Date & Sale Games.

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…

More from Andrew Highton

item.title: Call of Duty: Black Ops 1 and Black Ops 2 Rated in Korea
item.content:encoded:
The first couple of Black Ops games were very special. The campaigns, the Zombies, the Multiplayer; everything about the first two BLOPS games just clicked. In an interesting turn of events, both Black Ops 1 and Black Ops 2 have been rated again, this time in Korea.

We’re fresh off the Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 4 reveal and release date.

So, it’s fair to say that not many of you might have been ready for two classic CoD games to suddenly be rated. However, the Korean Game Content Rating Board has revealed that Black Ops 1 and 2 have been certified in 2026!

Black Ops 1 and Black Ops 2 Rated in South Korea

On May 29, it was discovered that Call of Duty: Black Ops and Call of Duty: Black Ops 2 had both been given a Game Rating Classification Decision by the GRAC.

There doesn’t appear to be anything different compared to the original games and ratings.

They are both deemed ‘Not for Teenagers’ and outline the same plots from both games, and instances of swear words, drug mentions, and more.

Nothing else is revealed, but this does suggest that Activision could be making some moves with older CoD games. We’ve had Modern Warfare remasters in the past, and there’s a chance we could get even more remasters, or the legendary titles could be getting a Nintendo Switch 2 port.

Call of Duty’s Nintendo march is heating up after we learned that Modern Warfare 4 will launch on Nintendo Switch 2.

What do you think the ratings mean? Are we getting more remasters? Or will they be simple campaign ports for the Nintendo Switch 2? Let us know what you think and what’s coming through the Insider Gaming Discord.

Check out more IG news:

  • ARC Raiders Finally Wins a Game of the Year Award
  • Rayman Legends Retold Price Revealed
  • Modern Warfare 4 Cosmetics & Collabs Will Be ‘Grounded’

Written by
